WOMAN'S ClVlCS CLUB TO


FORM TONIGHT


A meeting of the former menn-
bers of the Woman's Suffrage So
ciety will be held in the Cham
Der of Commerce assembly rooms
tonight at 1880. The COMMITTEES
CHI n to frame q constitution and
bylaws for the new club will SUBJ
NIT their report, an organization
will be perfected, name chosen
and officers elected. Also at the
meeting this evening will be de-
cided the question as to whether
the club will be for women only OF
for the mutual benefit of men and
women. A discussion of the com.
mission form of government will be
held impromptu and the commits
sion booklet will be read.


. 4 q


SPOKANE soRosIs MEETS TO.


DAY.


The Spokane Sorosis will meet
at the home of Mrs. Samuel Offner,
1913 Third avenue, this afternoon.
American literature Is under dis
cussion by the club and today two
writer's, Jewett and Freeman will
be discussed.


= = =


SCHUBERT CLUB IN Benefit.
The Schubert club, Spokane's
latest musical organization, viii
make its first appearance at the
Auditorium theater Friday evening,
the proceeds being turned over to
the Associated Charities of this
city. Tonight a rehearsal will be
held in the Sherman Clay hall and
on Friday, between IL and I
o'clock, the final rehearsal will DO
held in the theater building.


& & =


RED MEN PLAN DANCE.


The Improved Order of Red Men
will dance at the Elk's temple on
December s. The affair, which Is
the first of the season, will be in
the nature of benefit for the
Peone tribe of Hillyard. The han
is to be decorated in Indian style
and prominent Indians of the past
will be portrayed by the members.
